MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MATTHEW D. GRAMLY, ESQ., 415.421.2800, FOR PLAINTIFF JOSE PASCUAL.
BRAD J. VORNHOLT, ESQ., 4925.280.0004, FOR DEFENDANTS JIMMY DIAMOND GLASS, INC., HOA TANG AND CHUONG CHAU.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs JIMMY DIAMOND GLASS, INC., HOA TANG, CHUONG CHAU's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for summary judgment is granted as to the individual defendant Chau. Corporate directors cannot be held vicariously liable for the corporation's torts in which they do not participate. Their liability, if any, stems from their own tortious conduct, not from their status as directors or officers of the enterprise. (Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490, 503.) Plaintiffs do not dispute that Chau is the president and sole owner of Jimmy Diamond Glass Company and was not present at the jobsite and did not provide any supervision of the work performed on the date of the accident. The motion is denied on all other grounds. There is a triable issue of fact regarding whether Defendant Jimmy Diamond Glass Company had the right to control the activities of decedent thereby making decedent a temporary employee for purposes of the worker's compensation law. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
